english: zúr-tsam (zur tsam), to contract, compress, abridge, frq., de yaṅ bsdú-na (de yang bsdu na) if one shortens it still more, if it is abridged a second time Gram.; *dŭs-kaṅ* (*dus kyang*) W. brief, concise, compendious; *dŭ-yig* (*du yig*) C. abbreviation, abridgment; bsdus-`grel (bsdus 'grel) an abridged commentary Tar. 177,7; to close, conclude, finish, terminate, mjuq sdúd-pa (mjug sdud pa) to close a train, opp. to sna `drén-pa (sna 'dren pa) Mng.; slár-bsdu-ba (slar bsdu ba) concluding a sentence or period with the finite verb in o, Gram. — 4. bsdús-pa (bsdus pa) to consist of or in, c. instrum., e.g. yi-ge drug-gis (yi ge drug gis) of six letters Thgy. -- 5. to boil down, to inspissate Lt., bsdús-ku (bsdus khu), ydús-ku (gdus khu), a preparation thus obtained Med.; bsdus-táṅ (bsdus thang) prob. id. Med. — 6. scil. bsód-nams (bsod nams): bsdú-ba rnam bżi (bsdu ba rnam bzhi) the four ways of collecting merit Glr. — 7. dbugs sdúd-pa (dbugs sdud pa) Med.? bsdú-ba (bsdu ba) sbst. collection, gathering Tar. 33, 16. -- bsdus-yżom (bsdus gzhom) or `jom ('jom) Schr.: a machine for executing criminals constructed in such a manner, that the head is crushed by two stones striking together; Stg.: n. of one of the hells

1. vb., pf. bsdums, fut. bsdum, imp. sdum(s), vb.a. to `dúm-pa ('dum pa), to make agree, to bring to an agreement, mi-mtún-pa-rnams (mi mthun pa rnams) things not agreeing Sch., to reconcile, to conciliate, mi-mdzá-ba-rnams (mi mdza' ba rnams) enemies Thgy.; sdúm-par byéd-pa (sdum par byed pa) id.; sdun-byéd (sdun byed) (resp. mdzad) (mdzad), sdúm(-pa)-po (sdum pa po), sdúm-mk̇an (sdum mkhan), conciliator, pacifier, peacemaker; res `k̇rúgs-pa (res 'khrugs pa) res bsdúm-pa maṅ-du byuṅ-ṅo (res bsdum pa mang du byung ngo) at one time they were at odds, at another they were at peace with one another Tar. -- 2. sbst. house, mansion C.; yzim-sdum (gzim sdum) (resp.) bed-room; sdúm-ra (sdum ra) garden near the house, cf. ldúm-ra (ldum ra).

pf. and fut. bsdur, to compare, go-sdúr byéd-pa (go sdur byed pa) id., v. go 2; nyams sdur byéd-pa (nyams sdur byed pa) C. to compare different texts; *taṅ-dúr* (*tang sdur*) W judicial examination, trial.

(Ssk. in compound words) part, portion, of a whole, e.g. of a country, also yúl-sde (yul sde), province, district, territory, even village C., bón-sde (bon sde) the places or villages of the Bonpas Glr.; sde-čéṅ-la snyég-pa (sde chen la snyeg pa) to aim at an extension of territory Dom.; part of the human race: nation, people, tribe, clan, community, pá-rol-gyi sde `jóms-pa (pha rol gyi sde 'joms pa) to conquer hostile nations; class, e.g. of letters: phonetical class; sde sder bgó-ba (sde sder bgo ba) to divide into classes Cs.; classes of books: mdó-sde (mdo sde) the Sūtras, v. sub mdo; rgyúd-sde (rgyud sde) the Tantras, v. sub rgyud; sbyór-sde bżi (sbyor sde bzhi) the four volumes treating of pharmacy Glr.; of monks: community of monks, body of conventuals (consisting of not less than four persons); hence convent, monastery, sde btsugs (sde btsugs) he founded convents Glr.; čos-sde (chos sde) id.; class of religious followers, philosophical school, sde bżi (sde bzhi) the four (principal) schools Tar.; lha srín-gyi sde brgyad (lha srin gyi sde brgyad), lha klú-la sógs-pai sde brgyad (lha klu la sogs pai sde brgyad) the eight classes of spirits, frq.; it is also used for a great quantity, great many, lots of; and by improper use, or by way of abbreviation for sdé-pa (sde pa), sde-dpón (sde dpon), commander, ruler. Comp. and deriv. sde-skór (sde skor) Glr. district. -- sde-krugs (sde khrugs) insurrection, general revolt of a people, byéd-pa (byed pa) to excite one Ma. -- sde-snód gsum (sde snod gsum), त्रिपिटक, 'the three baskets', viz. the three classes of the sacred Buddhist writings, `dúl-bai ('dul bai) (discipline), mdo-sdéi (mdo sdei) (Sūtras), sṅágs-kyi sde-snód (sngags kyi sde snod) (Mantras, i.e. metaphysics and mysticism), hence sde-snód-la sbyáṅ-ba (sde snod la sbyang ba) to study the sacred writings Mil. -- sdé-pa (sde pa) 1. the chief or governor of a district C., = `gó-pa ('go pa) W., majordomo of the Dalai Lama, Köpp. II., 134; in a general sense: a man of quality, a nobleman Ma. 2. a letter of a certain phonetic class, or the phonetic class itself, sdé-pa bżi-pa (sde pa bzhi pa) the fourth phonetic class, the labials Gram. So the word is also used for denoting a certain class or school of Buddhist philosophers, Tar., frq. -- sde-dpón (sde dpon) = sdé-pa (sde pa) 1, signifies also a class of demons Dom. -- sde-tsán (sde tshan) class, e.g. phonetic class, = sde; a particular kind of writing, nā-ga-ri sde-tsán (sde tshan) Glr.; -- sde-yzár (sde gzar) Sch. lawlessness, anarchy, sde-yzár čéṅ-po (sde gzar chen po) general anarchy (?) -- sde-yaṅs (sde yangs) (spelling?) court, court-yard, = k̇yams (khyams). -- sde-rigs (sde rigs) dominion, territory,
